name: claude-code-resume
title: Claude Code Resume
description: Resume any past Claude Code session, not just the last one. Browse your full session history with recall (title, first and latest prompt, last reply) and jump back in — on macOS and Windows (WSL and native).
author: izumiz

commands.name: list-sessions
commands.title: Resume Claude Code Session
commands.subtitle: Claude Code
commands.description: Browse your Claude Code session history (newest first) and resume any session in its original directory.

commands.name: open-project
commands.title: Open Claude Code Project
commands.subtitle: Claude Code
commands.description: Pick a recent project and start Claude Code in that directory — a new session or continue the last one.

commands.name: setup
commands.title: Check Claude Code Setup
commands.subtitle: Claude Code
commands.description: Verify the resolved paths and the claude binary, and open preferences to override them.

preferences.name: claudeHome
preferences.title: Claude Home (.claude)
preferences.description: Leave empty to auto-detect (~/.claude on macOS; on Windows the WSL home is queried automatically). Set a UNC path only to override, e.g. \\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu\home\you\.claude.

preferences.name: claudeBin
preferences.title: Claude Binary
preferences.description: Use "claude" if it is on your PATH, or provide an absolute path.

preferences.name: wslDistro
preferences.title: WSL Distro
preferences.description: Windows only. e.g. Ubuntu (check with: wsl -l -q).

preferences.name: winShell
preferences.title: Windows Shell
preferences.description: Windows only. Shell used to launch Windows-native (non-WSL) sessions.

preferences.name: macTerminal
preferences.title: macOS Terminal
preferences.description: macOS only. Which terminal app to launch interactive sessions in.
